sourceMovie.title: Viral
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Viral works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 28 Days Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Viral clicked for you, 28 Days Later (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Contagion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Viral clicked for you, Contagion (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Viral?
faq.answer: Try 28 Days Later, Contagion, 28 Weeks Later—each shares DNA with Viral while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Viral?
faq.answer: Viral (2016) is best described as Drama, Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Viral worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
